name: Molino Viejo de Los Álamos a Pico del Águila — circular desde Arrabal de Portillo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Portillo offers routes through a high-alpine environment characterized by towering peaks and dramatic landscapes. The region features a mix of challenging mountain passes, river valleys, and forested sections. Trails often traverse open plains and ascend towards prominent local hills, providing diverse riding experiences. The area's geography includes significant elevation changes, making for demanding yet rewarding mountain bike routes. Best mountain bike trails around Portillo The most popular mountain bike trail is Molino Viejo de Los Álamos a Pico del Águila — circular desde Arrabal de Portillo, a 28.9 miles (46.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route explores varied terrain, including open plains and ascents towards Pico del Águila. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Llano de San Marugán and Montes de La Parrilla Loop from Arrabal de Portillo, a difficult 26.8 miles (43.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and panoramic views across the landscape. Local mountain bikers also love the Cega River Trails – Bridge Over the Cega loop from Portillo, a 46.3 miles (74.4 km) trail leading through river valleys and forested areas, often completed in about 5 hours 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Portillo is defined by high-altitude terrain, river valleys, and pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Portillo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Portillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Portillo?
A: There are 12 mountain bike trails around Portillo, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These routes explore diverse terrain, including high-alpine environments, river valleys, and forested sections.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Portillo?
A: While Portillo is primarily known as a winter ski resort, the warmer months are ideal for mountain biking. The region's high-alpine environment means summer offers the most favorable conditions for exploring the trails and enjoying the dramatic landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Portillo?
A: The trails around Portillo are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no routes specifically listed as easy. Riders should be prepared for significant elevation changes and challenging terrain, characteristic of a high-alpine environment.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Portillo's mountain bike trails?
A: Portillo's mountain bike trails feature a mix of high-alpine terrain, river valleys, and pine forests. You'll encounter open plains, ascents towards prominent local hills, and routes traversing varied landscapes with significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1178385490/molino-viejo-de-los-alamos-a-pico-del-aguila-circular-desde-arrabal-de-portillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Viejo de Los Álamos a Pico del Águila — circular desde Arrabal de Portillo&lt;/a&gt; route explores open plains and ascends towards Pico del Águila.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Portillo?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Portillo are circular. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1178385490/molino-viejo-de-los-alamos-a-pico-del-aguila-circular-desde-arrabal-de-portillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino Viejo de Los Álamos a Pico del Águila — circular desde Arrabal de Portillo&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1178353319/llano-de-san-marugan-and-montes-de-la-parrilla-loop-from-arrabal-de-portillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llano de San Marugán and Montes de La Parrilla Loop from Arrabal de Portillo&lt;/a&gt; are both loop trails.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific viewpoints on the mountain bike trails are not detailed, the region itself is known for its dramatic Andean landscapes and the stunning Laguna del Inca. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2689667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cega River Trails&lt;/a&gt; offer picturesque river valley scenery. The iconic Los Caracoles road, though primarily paved, offers spectacular views of the Andean landscape and Laguna del Inca.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Portillo?
A: The mountain bike routes in Portillo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to pine forests, and the challenging yet rewarding nature of the routes.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, there are several interesting landmarks nearby. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Castle&lt;/a&gt; or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5597863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Megeces Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31947615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Castle – Tudela de Duero Bridge loop from Portillo&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the castle.

Q: Are there opportunities for off-road mountain biking or singletrack trails?
A: The guide highlights routes that traverse varied terrain, including open plains, river valleys, and forested sections, indicating off-road opportunities. However, specific details on dedicated singletrack or downhill runs within the immediate Portillo ski resort area are not prominently documented, with much of the region's cycling fame stemming from the challenging paved Los Caracoles road.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in Portillo?
A: The longest mountain bike trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34484865" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cega River Trails – Bridge Over the Cega loop from Portillo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 46.3 miles (74.4 km) and typically takes around 5 hours and 37 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the mountain bike trails?
A: Information on specific cafes directly along the mountain bike trails is not available. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the high-altitude environment. The iconic Los Caracoles road, for example, is known for a lack of refilling stations.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on Portillo's mountain bike trails?
A: The trails in Portillo feature significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38292117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail through the Pine Forest – Tudela de Duero Bridge loop from Bodón de las Palomas&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of approximately 563 meters, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31947615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo Castle – Tudela de Duero Bridge loop from Portillo&lt;/a&gt; has over 600 meters of ascent.
tips: Viewpoint to the plain and agricultural fields around Megeces and to the forests of Mount Saint Marugan.

waypoints.name: View of the Plains and Fields Near Megeces from Pico del Águila
